description: Multi-agent MedQA system with RAG and cross-session memory — LM Studio local models, English textbooks, markdown-based memory, multi-source evaluation

features.name: Add LangChain Dependencies
features.description: Add langchain, langchain-chroma, langchain-text-splitters, and langchain-openai to pyproject.toml for RAG and LM Studio integration
features.evidence: uv sync --all-groups succeeded; langchain 1.3.11, langchain-chroma 1.1.0, langgraph 1.2.7, chromadb 1.5.9, sentence-transformers 5.6.0 installed

features.name: Create source directory structure
features.description: Create src/medqa_multi_agents/ with: prompts/ (markdown prompt files), vectorstore/ (ChromaDB module), agents/ (agent-wrapped nodes), memory/ (short-term and long-term memory modules), and scripts/ (ingestion). Each sub-package gets an __init__.py.
features.evidence: src/medqa_multi_agents/{prompts,vectorstore,agents,scripts}/ created with __init__.py files; memory/ deferred to memory-short-term feature

features.name: Extract all prompts into markdown files
features.description: Create src/medqa_multi_agents/prompts/ with 5 markdown files: supervisor.md (supervisor agent system prompt — decides routing), rewriter.md (rewriter agent — uses with_structured_output(RewrittenQuery) to produce structured rewritten query), retriever.md (retriever agent — searches Chroma and returns context), answerer.md (answerer agent — synthesizes answer from context), and evaluator.md (evaluator agent — returns verdict: correct / incorrect / incomplete + reasoning). Each prompt is loaded as a string at runtime — no hard-coded prompt strings in Python code. get_latest_news is excluded (not needed for MedQA textbook Q&A).
features.evidence: Created prompts/{supervisor,rewriter,retriever,answerer,evaluator}.md — all loaded via read_file at runtime

features.name: Build persistent ChromaDB vector store module
features.description: Implement src/medqa_multi_agents/vectorstore/ with: (1) embedding.py — HuggingFaceEmbeddings wrapper (CUDA-accelerated, batch_size=32); (2) db.py — Chroma client with DEFAULT_PERSIST_DIR; (3) scripts/ingest_textbooks.py — spaCy sentencizer chunking, all-mpnet-base-v2 embeddings, 86493 chunks from 18 textbooks.
features.evidence: Persistent Chroma DB at src/medqa_multi_agents/vectorstore/.chroma_db/ with 86493 chunks; CUDA confirmed on RTX 5060 Ti

features.name: Create rewriter agent
features.description: Create src/medqa_multi_agents/agents/rewriter.py. Use create_agent with the shared model and system_prompt loaded from prompts/rewriter.md. The rewriter agent uses with_structured_output(RewrittenQuery) where RewrittenQuery is a Pydantic BaseModel with a query str field. Wrap as @tool retrieve_documents that takes a question string and returns the rewritten query. Tool returns only the final text response.
features.evidence: 11/11 pytest tests pass (tests/test_rewriter.py): schema/unit (no LM Studio), direct with_structured_output, and end-to-end @tool invoke; qwen3-8b LM Studio confirmed working; rewriter uses model.with_structured_output directly inside @tool (create_agent wrapper removed — broke qwen3-8b tool-calling)

features.name: Create retriever agent
features.description: Create src/medqa_multi_agents/agents/retriever.py. Use create_agent with the shared model and system_prompt loaded from prompts/retriever.md. The retriever is a full agent — it decides search strategy, invokes the Chroma retriever internally, and returns context strings. Wrap as @tool retrieve_context that takes a rewritten query and returns context. Tool returns only the final text response.
features.evidence: 7/7 pytest tests pass (tests/test_retriever.py): schema/unit (no LM Studio), tool signature, and end-to-end Chroma integration; vectorstore cached at module level to avoid GPU OOM on re-load; qwen3-8b LM Studio confirmed working

features.name: Create answerer agent
features.description: Create src/medqa_multi_agents/agents/answerer.py. Use create_agent with the shared model and system_prompt loaded from prompts/answerer.md. Wrap as @tool answer_question that takes context and original question, returns the generated answer text. Tool returns only the final text response.
features.evidence: 7/7 pytest tests pass (tests/test_answerer.py): schema/unit (no LM Studio), tool signature, and end-to-end integration; model.with_structured_output(Answer) inside @tool (same qwen3-8b-compatible pattern as rewriter/retriever); answerer tool takes context+question as separate args

features.name: Create evaluator agent with revision loop
features.description: Add an evaluator agent that judges the answerer agent's answer for correctness and completeness. Implemented in src/medqa_multi_agents/agents/evaluator.py: create_agent with prompts/evaluator.md, wrapped as @tool evaluate_answer. The supervisor workflow routes answerer → evaluator. If evaluator returns 'incorrect' or 'incomplete', supervisor issues a 'reconsider' directive to answerer with the evaluator's reasoning, forming a revision loop. Maximum 2 loops (configurable) to prevent infinite loops. Produces a verdict string (correct / incorrect / incomplete) and reasoning text.
features.evidence: 8/8 pytest tests pass (tests/test_evaluator.py): schema/unit (no LM Studio), tool signature, and end-to-end integration with LM Studio; model.with_structured_output(EvaluationResult) inside @tool (same qwen3-8b-compatible pattern as rewriter/retriever/answerer); evaluator tool takes draft_answer+question+context as separate args; verdict+reasoning returned as JSON string

features.name: Implement supervisor workflow core with revision loop (no memory)
features.description: Refactor src/medqa_multi_agents/__init__.py to use the supervisor pattern with evaluator revision loop using LangGraph StateGraph + conditional edges. supervisor_agent uses tools [retrieve_documents, answer_question, evaluate_answer]. State fields: question (str), rewritten_query (str), documents (list[str]), draft_answer (str), evaluation_result (str), revision_count (int), evaluation_reasoning (str), final_answer (str). Execution: (1) START → supervisor decides next, (2) supervisor calls retrieve_documents → rewrite_query → retrieve (3-way), (3) supervisor calls answer_question, (4) supervisor calls evaluate_answer, (5) conditional edge: if verdict is 'incorrect'/'incomplete' and revision_count < MAX_REVISION_LOOPS → route back to answerer with evaluator feedback; else → END. MAX_REVISION_LOOPS defaults to 2. Load prompts from prompts/*.md at module load time. Use Chroma vectorstore from vectorstore/db.py. Shared model instance for all agents. NO checkpointer, NO store, NO recall_memory tool. Workflow invoked identically: workflow.invoke({'question': '...'}). Final answer printed to stdout. This feature is completable and testable independently of any memory features.
features.evidence: 16/16 pytest tests pass (tests/test_supervisor.py): State schema, routing logic, node functions, graph compilation, end-to-end invoke with mock tools and revision loop boundary; 49/49 total tests pass (full suite); revision_count incremented in _node_answer when verdict is incorrect/incomplete; MAX_REVISION_LOOPS=2; workflow and invoke exported

features.name: Wire memory into supervisor workflow and update supervisor prompt
features.description: Update src/medqa_multi_agents/__init__.py to wire both memory systems via the single ENABLE_MEMORY flag on top of supervisor-workflow-core. When ENABLE_MEMORY=true: (1) StateGraph compiled with checkpointer for short-term memory; (2) recall_memory tool added to supervisor tool list for long-term memory; (3) prompts/supervisor.md updated so the supervisor knows when to call recall_memory to check for past relevant sessions before answering, and when to write new sessions to the store after final answer. When ENABLE_MEMORY=false: no checkpointer, no store, no recall_memory tool — completely disabled. Depends on supervisor-workflow-core, memory-short-term, and memory-long-term all being complete.
features.evidence: Session 009: __init__.py wires (1) InMemorySaver checkpointer via get_checkpointer() when ENABLE_MEMORY=true, (2) dedicated 'recall' node (START→recall→rewrite_retrieve) that calls recall_memory tool, (3) _node_finalize calls save_session to persist session. prompts/supervisor.md already documents recall_memory usage. 46/46 non-skipped tests pass. ENABLE_MEMORY=false path tested: recall node returns empty string, save_session is a no-op, no store created.

features.name: Add textbook ingestion script
features.description: Create src/medqa_multi_agents/scripts/ingest_textbooks.py as a runnable entry point. Loads all 18 English textbooks from datasets/MedQA-USMLE/textbooks/en/, splits with spaCy sentencizer (4 lines per paragraph, 10 sentences per chunk, min 30 tokens), embeds via all-mpnet-base-v2 on CUDA (batch_size=32), and upserts into the persistent Chroma DB. Run with 'make ingest'.
features.evidence: Full ingest confirmed: 86493 chunks from 18 textbooks written to src/medqa_multi_agents/vectorstore/.chroma_db/; CUDA confirmed on RTX 5060 Ti

features.name: Add short-term memory (checkpointer) to supervisor graph
features.description: Add thread-level persistence to the supervisor StateGraph using a checkpointer. When ENABLE_MEMORY=true (default), the compiled StateGraph is compiled with a PostgresCheckpointer (or InMemorySaver for dev). Each MedQA question session uses a unique thread_id so the full revision loop state (draft_answer, evaluation_result, revision_count, messages) is persisted across turns. When ENABLE_MEMORY=false, no checkpointer is attached — the graph is stateless per invocation.
features.evidence: Windows/CUDA verification Session 009: uv sync --all-groups installed 182 packages (torch==2.11.0+cu128 confirmed on RTX 5060 Ti); uv run pytest tests/test_supervisor.py -v → 20/20 passed; uv run pytest -v → 32 passed, 21 skipped. ENABLE_MEMORY flag, InMemorySaver checkpointer, build_thread_config all verified on Windows Python 3.12.2.

features.name: Add long-term memory — frozen role-specific rule store
features.description: Replaced InMemoryStore Q&A session cache with a frozen JSON rule store (memory/long_term_rules.json). LongTermMemory class (src/medqa_multi_agents/memory/long_term.py) implements load(), search(), get_rules_for_agent(), add_rule(), save(). search() uses deterministic keyword-overlap scoring with metadata filters (agent/topic/memory_type/tags). Default read_only=True prevents writes during official eval. Module-level singleton long_term_memory loaded from memory/long_term_rules.json. 9 initial rules across 5 agent roles. No Q&A pairs, no gold answers, no answer_idx stored.
features.evidence: Session 010: LongTermMemory class implemented; memory/long_term_rules.json (9 rules); config.yaml; logs/dev_traces/ and logs/official_traces/ directories; build_rules_from_dev_logs.py offline script. 36/36 new tests in tests/test_long_term_memory.py pass (7 required scenarios + unit tests). Full suite pending.

features.name: V3 graph: load_memory node injects frozen rules into agent prompts
features.description: Replaced _node_recall (Q&A store) with _node_load_memory (frozen rule retrieval). State gains global_memory, retrieval_memory, reasoner_memory, verifier_memory list fields. load_memory node (START→load_memory→rewrite_retrieve→...) calls get_rules_for_agent() for each role when ENABLE_MEMORY=true; returns empty lists when false (V2 behaviour). Agent nodes (_node_rewrite_retrieve, _node_answer, _node_evaluate) format memory as prompt sections. _node_finalize no longer calls save_session (read-only memory design). prompts/rewriter.md, answerer.md, evaluator.md updated with memory guidance sections.
features.evidence: Session 010: 24/24 supervisor tests pass; 36/36 LTM tests pass. test_save_session_no_longer_exported, test_state_no_past_sessions_field, test_node_load_memory_returns_lists all pass. ENABLE_MEMORY flag gates both checkpointer and rule injection.
